reasoning: The question asks if 'Riots' in Colombia will exceed a 10-fold increase over the current historic 30-day average (~22.8), meaning more than ~228 riots would need to occur within a 30-day window prior to each resolution date (approx. 7.6 riots per day). According to recent news and ACLED data, while there are ongoing political tensions, armed group fighting (battles), and pro-government/anti-government protests (such as those on March 18-19, 2025 regarding Gustavo Petro's reforms), there is no evidence of a massive, nationwide uprising characterized specifically by 'Riots' (which ACLED strictly categorizes separately from peaceful 'Protests' or armed 'Battles'). Hitting a 10x multiplier over a full month is an extraordinarily high bar that would require historical levels of sustained daily violence akin to the peaks of the 2021 national strike. In mid-April 2025, there is no such uprising underway, making the nearest dates highly improbable. The probability slightly increases for October due to reported plans for a popular referendum on labor reforms which could spark unrest, though reaching the 10x threshold remains very unlikely.
